docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Trustless NFT Ecosystem
docs.desc: 10,000 fully on-chain generative NFTs with staking rewards
docs.manifestoIntro: Dunk Poge embodies the principles outlined in
docs.noSecretsDesc: NFT generation uses public blockchain data. No private keys control user assets after deployment.

principles.title: Self-Sovereignty
principles.desc: Every user authorizes their own actions. No one mints, stakes, or claims on your behalf.

principles.title: Verifiability
principles.desc: All NFT metadata lives on-chain as SVG. Anyone can verify what happened from public data.

principles.title: Censorship Resistance
principles.desc: No admin can prevent unstaking or claiming rewards.

principles.title: Walkaway Test
principles.desc: If the team disappears, the contracts continue functioning. NFTs remain yours forever.

principles.title: Accessibility
principles.desc: Mint price (0.005 ETH) and on-chain storage mean anyone can participate without servers or infrastructure.

principles.title: Transparency
principles.desc: Emission rates, decay curves, and multipliers are public constants. No hidden mechanics.

docs.rarityDesc: Actual rarity emerges from mint distribution. Attempting to game specific traits makes them more common, not rarer.

achievements.name: Collector
achievements.req: Hold 10+ NFTs staked for 7+ days at peak

guarantees.title: No External Dependencies
guarantees.desc: NFT metadata is entirely on-chain. No IPFS, no HTTP endpoints, no server dependencies.

guarantees.title: User Control
guarantees.desc: Your NFTs are always withdrawable. Pool depletion results in partial payment, not reverts.

guarantees.title: Verifiability
guarantees.desc: Anyone can verify rarity, pending rewards, and emission rates directly from contract code.

guarantees.title: Censorship Resistance
guarantees.desc: No admin can prevent unstaking or claiming rewards.

guarantees.title: Immutability
guarantees.desc: DunkPogeStaking has zero admin functions. Cannot be paused, upgraded, or modified.

guarantees.title: Emergency Recovery
guarantees.desc: emergencyWithdraw() always returns NFTs, even if rewards are forfeited.

patterns: Stack depth optimized for gas savings

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.headline: We Collect Nothing
privacy.why1: The entire application is a static frontend that interacts directly with Ethereum smart contracts.
privacy.why2: There is literally no backend to send data to.
privacy.publicDesc: When you interact with Ethereum, some information is publicly visible on-chain:
publicButItems: Is not on our servers (we have no servers)
Was your choice to create (you initiated the transactions)
privacy.sourceDesc: All code is open source on GitHub
privacy.runDesc: Clone the repo and run it on your own machine
privacy.cookiesNote: This data never leaves your browser. Clear it anytime in browser settings.
privacy.gdprDesc: No data = no compliance burden. No requests to handle, no exports, no deletions.
privacy.ccpaDesc: We have no data to sell, share, or delete.
privacy.otherLawsDesc: Laws regulate data collection. We don't collect any.
privacy.contactNote: Honestly, there's nothing to discuss. We don't collect anything.
privacy.phil1: If you shouldn't have to trust us with your assets (hence the trustless smart contracts), you shouldn't have to trust us with your data either.
privacy.phil2: This is what true decentralization looks like: we don't even know you exist unless you transact on-chain.
privacy.phil3: Even then, we only know that some wallet address interacted with a smart contract. We don't know that's you.
summaryItems: Blockchain data is public (inherent to Ethereum)
You can verify all of the above
privacy.summaryClose: That's it. That's the privacy policy.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.desc: Quick answers to common questions about minting, staking, and rewards
faq.joinCommunity: Join our community for support and updates

questions.q: How do I mint a Dunk Poge NFT?
questions.a: Connect your wallet, go to the Mint page, select a quantity (1-10), and click Mint Now. Each NFT costs 0.005 ETH. Traits are generated on-chain at mint time.
Maximum 10 NFTs per wallet during public sale. Total supply is fixed at 10,000.
Check the sale status on the Mint page. When it shows 'Sale Live' you can mint.
No. Traits are randomly generated from on-chain blockchain data (block hash, timestamp, etc.), ensuring fairness and preventing manipulation.

questions.q: How do I stake my NFTs?
questions.a: 1. Go to the Stake page. 2. Approve the staking contract (one-time). 3. Select the NFTs you want to stake. 4. Click Stake. Your NFTs transfer to the staking contract and immediately begin earning POGE.
Rewards accumulate every second based on the emission rate. You can claim at any time, or they're automatically sent when you unstake. No minimum waiting period.

questions.q: Do I lose my NFTs when staking?
questions.a: No. You can unstake at any time with no penalty. Use emergencyWithdraw() for instant return (forfeits rewards but immediately returns NFTs).
The longer you stake, the more you earn. Multiplier grows from 1x to 2x over 180 days. Example: Day 1 = 1x, Day 90 = 1.5x, Day 180+ = max 2x.

questions.q: Does claiming rewards reset my multiplier?
questions.a: No! The multiplier is based on stake duration, not claim frequency. Claiming rewards does not reset your loyalty progress.

questions.q: How much POGE can I earn?
questions.a: Base rate starts at ~10 POGE/day per NFT, decaying to ~1 POGE/day over 730 days. With the 2x multiplier, you can earn up to 20 POGE/day initially, tapering to 2 POGE/day.
POGE is the ecosystem token. Future utility TBD — currently distributed as staking rewards. Fixed supply of 1 billion tokens.

questions.q: What happens if the reward pool runs out?
questions.a: The contract handles low balance gracefully — it pays what's available and emits transparent events. 500M POGE is allocated to staking, providing a 27.6% safety buffer.
No. The staking contract is immutable with no admin functions. Emission rates are fixed in the code and cannot be changed.
Not formally audited. The contract uses battle-tested patterns: ERC721A, ReentrancyGuard, SafeERC20, and Checks-Effects-Interactions. Code is open source on Etherscan and GitHub for community review.

No. The staking contract has zero admin functions — no pause, no upgrade, no withdrawal. The NFT contract owner can only toggle sale status and withdraw mint funds. Your NFTs are always yours, staked or not.

questions.q: What if the website goes down?
questions.a: Everything works without the website. Interact directly with contracts via Etherscan. NFT metadata is fully on-chain — no IPFS or server dependency.
100% on-chain. The SVG and logic are embedded in the contract itself. No IPFS, no servers, no external dependencies. Your NFT exists as long as Ethereum does.

questions.q: Can my NFT be censored or frozen?
questions.a: No. Standard ERC721 with no blacklist or burn capabilities. Once minted, the NFT can be freely transferred, staked, or sold.

questions.q: My transaction failed — what do I do?
questions.a: Check: 1) Enough ETH for gas, 2) Sale is active when minting, 3) Contract is approved when staking, 4) Not exceeding limits. Try increasing gas limit if needed.
Approval is contract-specific. Make sure you approved the staking contract address shown on the stake page, not the NFT contract.
NFTs are SVG format — some wallets/marketplaces may need time to render. You can view directly via tokenURI() on the contract in Etherscan.
Yes, at any time. No lock-up period, no fees. Go to Stake page, select your NFTs, click Unstake. Rewards are automatically claimed.
